mappings.rationaleAI: Linguistics: Chomsky, structuralism, language acquisition (H, A.3.e).
Intelligence: IQ testing, nature-nurture, cognitive science (G).
Neuroscience: physiological psychology (G.3), Freudian convergences (A.3.c).
Hume: Enlightenment foundations, influence on Adam Smith (A.1.b, D.1).
Martineau: pioneered sociological methods, popularized economics (C.1, D.1).
Diamond's environmental determinism: anthropological methods (B.2).
Hypnosis: history and physiological psychology (G.1, G.3).

mappings.episodeTitle: The Royal Society and British Science: Episode 4
mappings.rationaleAI: Pitt-Rivers: emergence of anthropology as discipline (B.1, B.2).
Popper: critiqued Marxism and Freud as unfalsifiable (A.3.b, A.3.c).
The Individual: structuralism, Foucault (A.3.e, C.1).
Industrial Revolution's social upheaval stimulated sociology and economics.
Lenin: Marxism's influence on social sciences (A.3.b, E.1).
Malthus: political economy, demography (D.1, C.5).
Montesquieu: founding political science, Enlightenment heritage (E.1, A.1.b).
Psychoanalysis: Freudian influence on social sciences (A.3.c, G.1).
Ibn Khaldun: precursor of sociology and political science (A.1.a, C.1, E.1).
Lévi-Strauss: structuralist anthropology, linguistics intersection (B, A.3.e, H.2).
Childhood: developmental psychology, sociology of family (G.1, C.4).
Condorcet: Enlightenment heritage in social reform (A.1.b).
Herodotus: ethnographic methodology, precursor to anthropology (B.1).
Probability: quantitative methods in social sciences (A.3.d.iii, D.3).
Distinguishes biological from social gerontology, directly engaging with demography as a cognate discipline of sociology (C.5).
